setting.help: Define what makes articles relevant, uncertain, or discardable. The AI classifies each article into one of three outcomes: Relevant (auto-published to the blog), Uncertain (moved to Shortlist for human review), or Discard (moved to Discard stage and skipped).

div.pre:
You are curating a newsfeed for Swiss social sector professionals.
HIGHLY RELEVANT (auto-publish): - Policy changes affecting social institutions in Switzerland - Research findings on disability, integration, poverty, or social work - Funding announcements for social projects - Legal changes impacting NGOs or social services
MAYBE RELEVANT (needs human review): - General political news that might affect the social sector - Events and conferences in the social field - News from neighboring countries with Swiss implications
NOT RELEVANT (discard): - Entertainment, sports, lifestyle news - News from other countries without Swiss connection - Commercial press releases unrelated to social sector - Duplicate or very similar content to recent articles

setting.help: Define how teasers should be written for relevant articles.

div.pre:
Write a teaser for this news article following these guidelines:
- Length: 2-3 sentences (max 280 characters) - Tone: Professional but accessible, informative - Structure: Start with the key news, then why it matters - Language: Use the same language as the original article - Style: Neutral, factual, no clickbait or sensationalism - Goal: Help readers quickly decide if they want to read the full article

setting.help: Select which blog to publish curated articles to. Create a blog first if you don't have one.
Stage assigned to articles that need human review before publishing.
Stage assigned to articles that have been auto-published to the blog.
Stage assigned to articles that the pipeline has discarded as not relevant.
When articles don't have suitable header images, Pixabay can provide fallback images. Get a free API key at pixabay.com.

div.p: Get your free API key at pixabay.com/api/docs
